metadata.promotion_strategy: Derived MCQs from raw ASPE term-definition reference items

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any audible or visible signal indicating the existence of any condition or operation outside a preset normal.
questions.explanation_long: Alarm: Any audible or visible signal indicating the existence of any condition or operation outside a preset normal.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Hot and cold water mixed to obtain an intermediate temperature, generally from 80°F to 100°F (29°C to 43°C).
questions.explanation_long: Tempered water: Hot and cold water mixed to obtain an intermediate temperature, generally from 80°F to 100°F (29°C to 43°C).

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Piping that conveys corrosive or harmful industrial, chemical, or processed wastes to a separate drainage system.
questions.explanation_long: Chemical waste system: Piping that conveys corrosive or harmful industrial, chemical, or processed wastes to a separate drainage system.
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Chemical waste system

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The closeness of a public sewer to a building based on code requirements, generally 500 ft (151 m) or less.
questions.explanation_long: Availability of a sewer: The closeness of a public sewer to a building based on code requirements, generally 500 ft (151 m) or less.
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Availability of a sewer

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The temperature at which a material will ignite and sustain combustion in the absence of a spark or flame.
questions.explanation_long: Autoignition: The temperature at which a material will ignite and sustain combustion in the absence of a spark or flame.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Negative pressure created by any means in the potable water distribution system which causes a potential backflow.
questions.explanation_long: Backpressure: Negative pressure created by any means in the potable water distribution system which causes a potential backflow.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The degree of mutual agreement between individual measurements, namely repeatability and repro ducibility.
questions.explanation_long: Precision: The degree of mutual agreement between individual measurements, namely repeatability and repro ducibility.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any vent connecting a horizontal branch or fixture drain with the stack vent of the originating waste or soil stack.
questions.explanation_long: Vent, loop: Any vent connecting a horizontal branch or fixture drain with the stack vent of the originating waste or soil stack.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Comparison of the graduation of an instrument with a standard of known accuracy to eliminate variation.
questions.explanation_long: Calibration: Comparison of the graduation of an instrument with a standard of known accuracy to eliminate variation.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? All liquid or waterborne waste from industrial or commercial processes that has properties other than domestic sewage.
questions.explanation_long: Industrial waste: All liquid or waterborne waste from industrial or commercial processes that has properties other than domestic sewage.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Where internal water contact parts of valves known as trim materials (stem, disc, seat rings, etc.) are made of bronze.
questions.explanation_long: Bronzemounted: Where internal water contact parts of valves known as trim materials (stem, disc, seat rings, etc.) are made of bronze.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Clear wastewater drainage from equipment, rooms, and other areas that does not contain contaminants considered harmful.
questions.explanation_long: Clearwater waste: Clear wastewater drainage from equipment, rooms, and other areas that does not contain contaminants considered harmful.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Commonly called an angle stop, a valve used to control the water supply, usually to a single fixture.
questions.explanation_long: Stop valve: Commonly called an angle stop, a valve used to control the water supply, usually to a single fixture.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Several plumbing fixtures located together on the same level, generally a water closet, lavatory, and shower or bathtub.
questions.explanation_long: Bathroom group: Several plumbing fixtures located together on the same level, generally a water closet, lavatory, and shower or bathtub.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? These conversions denote the precise (or direct) conversion to the SI unit value, accurate to a number of decimal places.
questions.explanation_long: Exact: These conversions denote the precise (or direct) conversion to the SI unit value, accurate to a number of decimal places.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Soil piping with plain ends, with a joint consisting of a stainless steel or castiron clamp and neoprene gasket assembly.
questions.explanation_long: Hubless: Soil piping with plain ends, with a joint consisting of a stainless steel or castiron clamp and neoprene gasket assembly.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Sewage sediment, rich in destructive bacteria, that can be used to break down fresh sewage quickly.
questions.explanation_long: Activated sludge: Sewage sediment, rich in destructive bacteria, that can be used to break down fresh sewage quickly.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any pipe or fitting installed in a vertical position or that makes an angle of not more than 45 degrees with the vertical.
questions.explanation_long: Vertical pipe: Any pipe or fitting installed in a vertical position or that makes an angle of not more than 45 degrees with the vertical.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? When two wedges acting as a closure member in a gate valve are in contact with the seating faces.
questions.explanation_long: Double disc: When two wedges acting as a closure member in a gate valve are in contact with the seating faces.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The amount of heat required to raise the temperature of 1 pound (0.45 kg) of water 1°F (0.565°C).
questions.explanation_long: British thermal unit (Btu): The amount of heat required to raise the temperature of 1 pound (0.45 kg) of water 1°F (0.565°C).
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | British thermal unit (Btu)

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any fitting that mates or connects two pipes or fittings of different size, material, or design.
questions.explanation_long: Adapter fitting: Any fitting that mates or connects two pipes or fittings of different size, material, or design.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? An enclosure or chamber located at the curb that contains a shutoff valve on the supply line for gas or water to a building.
questions.explanation_long: Curb box: An enclosure or chamber located at the curb that contains a shutoff valve on the supply line for gas or water to a building.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Commonly called the rate of rainfall, the amount of rain measured in inches per hour (mm/hour).
questions.explanation_long: Rainfall intensity: Commonly called the rate of rainfall, the amount of rain measured in inches per hour (mm/hour).

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Regulations, subsequent amendments thereto, and any emergency rules that the department having jurisdiction may lawfully adopt.
questions.explanation_long: Code: Regulations, subsequent amendments thereto, and any emergency rules that the department having jurisdiction may lawfully adopt.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Furthest from the source of supply in terms of total pressure lost through the entire system.
questions.explanation_long: Hydraulically remote: Furthest from the source of supply in terms of total pressure lost through the entire system.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Conforming to the temperature and pressure requirements of the authority having jurisdiction.
questions.explanation_long: Standard temperature and pressure: Conforming to the temperature and pressure requirements of the authority having jurisdiction.
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Standard temperature and pressure

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any liquid waste containing animal, vegetable, or chemical wastes in suspension or solution.
questions.explanation_long: Sewage: Any liquid waste containing animal, vegetable, or chemical wastes in suspension or solution.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Water primarily indented for direct human use, such as that supplied to plumbing fixtures.
questions.explanation_long: Domestic water: Water primarily indented for direct human use, such as that supplied to plumbing fixtures.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Equipment, products, or materials approved by a thirdparty testing organization and acceptable to the authority having jurisdiction.
questions.explanation_long: Listed: Equipment, products, or materials approved by a thirdparty testing organization and acceptable to the authority having jurisdiction.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? 1. The lowest point of a stack of vertical pipe. 2. A liquid with a pH of 7.0 or higher.
questions.explanation_long: Base: 1. The lowest point of a stack of vertical pipe. 2. A liquid with a pH of 7.0 or higher.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Occupancies with moderate to substantial amounts of flammable or combustible liquids or where shielding of combustibles is extensive.
questions.explanation_long: Extra hazard group 2: Occupancies with moderate to substantial amounts of flammable or combustible liquids or where shielding of combustibles is extensive.
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Extra hazard group 2

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Material used to replace excavated material for piping installed in an earthen trench.
questions.explanation_long: Backfill: Material used to replace excavated material for piping installed in an earthen trench.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Common term for rightangle valves used to control water supplies to plumbing fixtures.
questions.explanation_long: Angle stop: Common term for rightangle valves used to control water supplies to plumbing fixtures.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The concentration of a rainstorm, used in the design of a stormwater drainage system.
questions.explanation_long: Duration: The concentration of a rainstorm, used in the design of a stormwater drainage system.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Hot water for other than potable use, intended for commercial or industrial purposes.
questions.explanation_long: Service hot water: Hot water for other than potable use, intended for commercial or industrial purposes.
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Service hot water

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any pipe that carries wastewater or waterborne wastes in a building drainage system.
questions.explanation_long: Drain: Any pipe that carries wastewater or waterborne wastes in a building drainage system.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any material in direct contact with a pipe that is under and up to its centerline.
questions.explanation_long: Bedding: Any material in direct contact with a pipe that is under and up to its centerline.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The formation of bubbles in a liquid because of a partial vacuum, which damages adjacent parts when these bubbles revert back to a liquid.
questions.explanation_long: Cavitation: The formation of bubbles in a liquid because of a partial vacuum, which damages adjacent parts when these bubbles revert back to a liquid.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Rainwater, snow, ice, or other natural precipitation from site or roof surfaces.
questions.explanation_long: Stormwater: Rainwater, snow, ice, or other natural precipitation from site or roof surfaces.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Two changes of direction installed in succession, or series, in continuous pipe.
questions.explanation_long: Double offset: Two changes of direction installed in succession, or series, in continuous pipe.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Regarding plastic pipe, the diameter of the pipe divided by the wall thickness.
questions.explanation_long: Standard dimensional ratio: Regarding plastic pipe, the diameter of the pipe divided by the wall thickness.
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Standard dimensional ratio

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? These conversions denote that the product or material characteristics are physically changed from existing values to preferred SI unit values.
questions.explanation_long: Hard: These conversions denote that the product or material characteristics are physically changed from existing values to preferred SI unit values.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Surface water flowing into a catch basin, manhole, or other collection device.
questions.explanation_long: Inflow: Surface water flowing into a catch basin, manhole, or other collection device.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Also called infiltration, the rate that a liquid will flow deeper into a soil.
questions.explanation_long: Percolation: Also called infiltration, the rate that a liquid will flow deeper into a soil.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? These units are used to denote angles. There are two supplementary units: Quantity Unit Symbol Plane angle radian rad Solid angle steradian sr
questions.explanation_long: Supplementary units: These units are used to denote angles. There are two supplementary units: Quantity Unit Symbol Plane angle radian rad Solid angle steradian sr

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any waterproof material fitted over a surface where water is expected to run.
questions.explanation_long: Flashing: Any waterproof material fitted over a surface where water is expected to run.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Biological decomposition of organic matter with the production of illsmelling products, usually occurring when there is a deficiency of oxygen.
questions.explanation_long: Putrefaction: Biological decomposition of organic matter with the production of illsmelling products, usually occurring when there is a deficiency of oxygen.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Sometimes called vapor lock, a condition in which a gas is entrapped between two liquid surfaces in a conduit causing a stoppage or impediment.
questions.explanation_long: Air lock: Sometimes called vapor lock, a condition in which a gas is entrapped between two liquid surfaces in a conduit causing a stoppage or impediment.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any physical, chemical, or biological substance found in water that makes it unsuitable for the purpose intended or as a source of potable water.
questions.explanation_long: Impurity: Any physical, chemical, or biological substance found in water that makes it unsuitable for the purpose intended or as a source of potable water.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Drainage collected during periods of no rain that contains little or no groundwater by infiltra tion and no stormwater at the time of collection.
questions.explanation_long: Dryweather flow: Drainage collected during periods of no rain that contains little or no groundwater by infiltra tion and no stormwater at the time of collection.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Water directly discharged from the clouds as snow, rain, hail, or sleet.
questions.explanation_long: Precipitation: Water directly discharged from the clouds as snow, rain, hail, or sleet.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Change in the shape or size of a body produced by the action of stress.
questions.explanation_long: Strain: Change in the shape or size of a body produced by the action of stress.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The specific point in a piping network where a pipe size is calculated.
questions.explanation_long: Design point: The specific point in a piping network where a pipe size is calculated.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Piping material, usually cast iron, that is thicker than standard pipe.
questions.explanation_long: Extra heavy: Piping material, usually cast iron, that is thicker than standard pipe.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The International System of Units has three types of units, as follows.
questions.explanation_long: SI Units and Symbols: The International System of Units has three types of units, as follows.
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | SI Units and Symbols

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Equipment, products, or materials bearing a label of a listing agency.
questions.explanation_long: Labeled: Equipment, products, or materials bearing a label of a listing agency.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? 1. When used for plumbing, any drainage system that carries human waste. 2. When used for pharma ceutical purposes, a system that is clean or sterile.
questions.explanation_long: Sanitary: 1. When used for plumbing, any drainage system that carries human waste. 2. When used for pharma ceutical purposes, a system that is clean or sterile.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Water found at or below grade extending down to an impervious layer.
questions.explanation_long: Groundwater: Water found at or below grade extending down to an impervious layer.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Water of sufficient purity suitable for human use and meeting the quality standards and regula tions of the public health authority having jurisdiction.
questions.explanation_long: Potable water: Water of sufficient purity suitable for human use and meeting the quality standards and regula tions of the public health authority having jurisdiction.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Water pressure less than static pressure that varies with flow rate.
questions.explanation_long: Residual pressure: Water pressure less than static pressure that varies with flow rate.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any pipe that conveys the discharge of human or animal bodily waste.
questions.explanation_long: Soil pipe: Any pipe that conveys the discharge of human or animal bodily waste.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? An incremental value in metric units (SI) where the freezing point of water (0°C) and the boiling point of water (100°C) are divided into 100 divisions.
questions.explanation_long: Degree Celsius (formally Centigrade) (C): An incremental value in metric units (SI) where the freezing point of water (0°C) and the boiling point of water (100°C) are divided into 100 divisions.
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Degree Celsius (formally Centigrade) (C)

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Estimated flow or use expected under specific operating conditions.
questions.explanation_long: Demand: Estimated flow or use expected under specific operating conditions.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The gradual destruction of metal or other material by the abrasive or electromechanical action of liquids, gases, solids, or mixtures of these materials.
questions.explanation_long: Erosion: The gradual destruction of metal or other material by the abrasive or electromechanical action of liquids, gases, solids, or mixtures of these materials.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Air compression that allows an increase of temperature in the air.
questions.explanation_long: Adiabatic compression: Air compression that allows an increase of temperature in the air.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? An incremental value in English (IP) units, where the freezing point of water (32°F) and the boiling point of water (212°F) are divided into 180 divisions.
questions.explanation_long: Degree Fahrenheit (F): An incremental value in English (IP) units, where the freezing point of water (32°F) and the boiling point of water (212°F) are divided into 180 divisions.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Piping running to a lower elevation within the same floor level.
questions.explanation_long: Drop: Piping running to a lower elevation within the same floor level.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any part of a piping system other than a main, riser, or stack.
questions.explanation_long: Branch: Any part of a piping system other than a main, riser, or stack.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Capable of being extended or shaped. Most metals are malleable.
questions.explanation_long: Malleable: Capable of being extended or shaped. Most metals are malleable.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Reactions within a body resisting external forces acting on it.
questions.explanation_long: Stress: Reactions within a body resisting external forces acting on it.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Loss of liquid from a fixture trap due to the pressure differential between the inlet and outlet of a trap, often caused by the discharge of another fixture.
questions.explanation_long: Induced siphonage: Loss of liquid from a fixture trap due to the pressure differential between the inlet and outlet of a trap, often caused by the discharge of another fixture.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Nonpotable water used as the intake to any device or process, generally used to describe water supply from a natural source such as a river, lake, or stream.
questions.explanation_long: Raw water: Nonpotable water used as the intake to any device or process, generally used to describe water supply from a natural source such as a river, lake, or stream.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Occupancies with little or no flammable or combustible liquids.
questions.explanation_long: Extra hazard group 1: Occupancies with little or no flammable or combustible liquids.
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Extra hazard group 1

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The process of capturing and carrying away gases vented from a patient breathing circuit during the normal operation of gas anesthesia or analgesia equipment.
questions.explanation_long: Waste anesthetic gas disposal (WAGD): The process of capturing and carrying away gases vented from a patient breathing circuit during the normal operation of gas anesthesia or analgesia equipment.
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Waste anesthetic gas disposal (WAGD)

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? (re: bacteria) Living or active in the absence of free oxygen.
questions.explanation_long: Anaerobic: (re: bacteria) Living or active in the absence of free oxygen.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Commonly called a hub, that portion of a pipe that is sufficiently enlarged to receive the mating spigot end of another pipe for the purpose of making a joint.
questions.explanation_long: Bell: Commonly called a hub, that portion of a pipe that is sufficiently enlarged to receive the mating spigot end of another pipe for the purpose of making a joint.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Observation, sampling, or testing at designated locations.
questions.explanation_long: Monitoring: Observation, sampling, or testing at designated locations.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Organisms considered to be in the coli aerogenes group.
questions.explanation_long: Coliform: Organisms considered to be in the coli aerogenes group.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any substance entering or carried in a drainage system.
questions.explanation_long: Effluent: Any substance entering or carried in a drainage system.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Water not safe for drinking, personal, or culinary use.
questions.explanation_long: Nonpotable water: Water not safe for drinking, personal, or culinary use.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Air at any pressure greater than atmospheric pressure.
questions.explanation_long: Air, compressed: Air at any pressure greater than atmospheric pressure.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Air having a temperature of 60°F (15.6°C), with a pressure of 14.70 psia (101.4 kPa) and 0 percent humidity. These figures may be different outside the United States.
questions.explanation_long: Air, standard: Air having a temperature of 60°F (15.6°C), with a pressure of 14.70 psia (101.4 kPa) and 0 percent humidity. These figures may be different outside the United States.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Living or active only in the presence of free oxygen.
questions.explanation_long: Aerobic: Living or active only in the presence of free oxygen.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Fittings where the openings on either end are offset.
questions.explanation_long: Eccentric fittings: Fittings where the openings on either end are offset.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Air subject only to ambient atmospheric conditions.
questions.explanation_long: Air, free: Air subject only to ambient atmospheric conditions.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The organization, office, or individual responsible for approving equipment, materials, installation, or procedure. 24 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
questions.explanation_long: Authority having jurisdiction (AHJ): The organization, office, or individual responsible for approving equipment, materials, installation, or procedure. 24 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Authority having jurisdiction (AHJ)

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any digit necessary to define a value or quantity.
questions.explanation_long: Significant digit: Any digit necessary to define a value or quantity.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Occupancies or portions of other occupancies where the quantity and/or combustibility of contents is low, and fires with relatively low rates of heat release are expected.
questions.explanation_long: Light hazard: Occupancies or portions of other occupancies where the quantity and/or combustibility of contents is low, and fires with relatively low rates of heat release are expected.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Abbreviation for British thermal units per hour.
questions.explanation_long: Btuh: Abbreviation for British thermal units per hour.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Molecules that separate from a gas upon cooling.
questions.explanation_long: Condensate: Molecules that separate from a gas upon cooling.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Separation and measurements of component parts.
questions.explanation_long: Analysis: Separation and measurements of component parts.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Accepted or acceptable under an applicable specification or standard stated or cited for the proposed use under the procedures and authority of the administrative authority.
questions.explanation_long: Approved: Accepted or acceptable under an applicable specification or standard stated or cited for the proposed use under the procedures and authority of the administrative authority.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Pharmaceutical air used for life support, supplied from cylinders, bulk containers, or medical air compressors or reconstituted from oxygen USP and oilfree, dry nitrogen NF.
questions.explanation_long: Medical air: Pharmaceutical air used for life support, supplied from cylinders, bulk containers, or medical air compressors or reconstituted from oxygen USP and oilfree, dry nitrogen NF.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Flow rate measured in feet per second (m/sec).
questions.explanation_long: Velocity: Flow rate measured in feet per second (m/sec).

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Commonly called a hubandspigot joint for castiron soil pipe, a joint in which the spigot is inserted into the hub and the joint is then made tight by caulking or by a gasket.
questions.explanation_long: Bellandspigot joint: Commonly called a hubandspigot joint for castiron soil pipe, a joint in which the spigot is inserted into the hub and the joint is then made tight by caulking or by a gasket.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? 1. To check or reduce. 2. To deaden vibration.
questions.explanation_long: Dampen: 1. To check or reduce. 2. To deaden vibration.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any deviation from parallel flow in a pipe.
questions.explanation_long: Turbulence: Any deviation from parallel flow in a pipe.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any group of two or more adjacent fixtures.
questions.explanation_long: Battery of fixtures: Any group of two or more adjacent fixtures.
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Battery of fixtures

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Steel pipe that has not been galvanized.
questions.explanation_long: Black pipe: Steel pipe that has not been galvanized.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? One of the 10 arabic numerals (0 to 9).
questions.explanation_long: Digit: One of the 10 arabic numerals (0 to 9).

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Often used nomenclature for a faucet.
questions.explanation_long: Cock: Often used nomenclature for a faucet.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any piping running to a lower level.
questions.explanation_long: Down: Any piping running to a lower level.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any pipe that conveys potable water.
questions.explanation_long: Waterdistributing pipe: Any pipe that conveys potable water.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? 1. The maximum or minimum flow obtainable under given conditions of media, temperature, pressure, velocity, etc. 2. The volume of media that may be stored in a container or receptacle.
questions.explanation_long: Capacity: 1. The maximum or minimum flow obtainable under given conditions of media, temperature, pressure, velocity, etc. 2. The volume of media that may be stored in a container or receptacle.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? 1. A drainage receptacle with a raised rim and without a strainer or grate. 2. A floor drain incapable of receiving surface drainage (commonly used for equipment condensation drainage).
questions.explanation_long: Hub drain: 1. A drainage receptacle with a raised rim and without a strainer or grate. 2. A floor drain incapable of receiving surface drainage (commonly used for equipment condensation drainage).

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Piping containing only stormwater.
questions.explanation_long: Leader: Piping containing only stormwater.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Pressure less than atmospheric.
questions.explanation_long: Negative pressure: Pressure less than atmospheric.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Free air outside any structure.
questions.explanation_long: Open air: Free air outside any structure.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The field of low temperature.
questions.explanation_long: Cryogenic: The field of low temperature.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Fired and glazed earthenware.
questions.explanation_long: Vitrified clay: Fired and glazed earthenware.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Liquid leaking into a sewer.
questions.explanation_long: Infiltration: Liquid leaking into a sewer.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Capable of being ignited.
questions.explanation_long: Flammable: Capable of being ignited.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? These conversions denote the conversion to the SI unit value in the software only. The materials and products remain unchanged, and minimal rounding off to the nearest integer is usually applied.
questions.explanation_long: Soft: These conversions denote the conversion to the SI unit value in the software only. The materials and products remain unchanged, and minimal rounding off to the nearest integer is usually applied.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Occupancies or portions of other occupancies where combustibility is low, the quan tity of combustibles does not exceed 8 feet (2.4 m), and fires with moderate rates of heat release are expected.
questions.explanation_long: Ordinary hazard group 1: Occupancies or portions of other occupancies where combustibility is low, the quan tity of combustibles does not exceed 8 feet (2.4 m), and fires with moderate rates of heat release are expected.
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Ordinary hazard group 1

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Effluent discharge that is produced from food processing, food preparation, or other sources where grease, fats, and oils enter automatic dishwater prerinse stations, sinks, or other appurtenances.
questions.explanation_long: Greaseladen waste: Effluent discharge that is produced from food processing, food preparation, or other sources where grease, fats, and oils enter automatic dishwater prerinse stations, sinks, or other appurtenances.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? 1. The flow of water or other liquids, mixtures, or substances into the distributing pipes of a potable supply from any source other than that intended. 2. The reversal of flow in drainage systems.
questions.explanation_long: Backflow: 1. The flow of water or other liquids, mixtures, or substances into the distributing pipes of a potable supply from any source other than that intended. 2. The reversal of flow in drainage systems.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? One of four distinct types of alarms in Category 1 and Category 2 medical gas sys temsmaster alarms, area alarms, local alarms, and Category 3 alarmsas recognized by NFPA 99: Healthcare Facilities Code.
questions.explanation_long: Medical gas alarm system: One of four distinct types of alarms in Category 1 and Category 2 medical gas sys temsmaster alarms, area alarms, local alarms, and Category 3 alarmsas recognized by NFPA 99: Healthcare Facilities Code.
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Medical gas alarm system

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Brand name for a highsilicon alloy that is resistant to practically all corrosive wastes, with a silicon content of approximately 14.5 percent and acid resistant throughout the entire thickness of the metal.
questions.explanation_long: Durion: Brand name for a highsilicon alloy that is resistant to practically all corrosive wastes, with a silicon content of approximately 14.5 percent and acid resistant throughout the entire thickness of the metal.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Any substance that exists in the gaseous state at normal atmospheric temperature and pressure and is capable of being ignited and burned when mixed with the proper proportion of air, oxygen, or other oxidizers.
questions.explanation_long: Flammable gas: Any substance that exists in the gaseous state at normal atmospheric temperature and pressure and is capable of being ignited and burned when mixed with the proper proportion of air, oxygen, or other oxidizers.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The building supply pipe, waterdistributing pipes, and necessary connecting pipes, fittings, control valves, and all appurtenances carrying or supplying potable water in, or adjacent to, a building or premises.
questions.explanation_long: Water supply system: The building supply pipe, waterdistributing pipes, and necessary connecting pipes, fittings, control valves, and all appurtenances carrying or supplying potable water in, or adjacent to, a building or premises.
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Water supply system
